title: Day in the life of a college student at GCU
body: This is so motivational. I feel like this has inspired me to start doing more things that fufill my soul, like say go on a hike and listen to coldplay's "Viva la Vida" (what a song) although if it was up to me i probably would've picked something a little harder like panic at the disco... lol that's just cause i'm not one of those regular normal guys. other than that this routine seems pretty f***ing solid man. you got the haircare, you got the breakfast (chick fil a is homophobic but i'll let it slide this once), you got the study sesh, the workout, the class, you watched a lil movie (i know your ass isn't an amc stubs member OR on letterboxd but whatever) i mean yeah dude... not saying i'd follow it step by step but i can behind it. 6/10 (wouldn't watch more than 3 times)
author: @DiegoDono-j2o

title: pros and cons of attending Grand Canyon University

title: pros and cons of attending Grand Canyon University

The pros for me being an online student is being able to be a wife mom & work.  But GCU is my home for the next four years so I wanted to do some homework on feeling a bit more involved as if I was actually there.. & I love love chick-fil- a too your amazing thank you for the video!  I feel I love with GCU online learning because it gives me the opportunity to for success for my future career. Blessings, ❤

title: pros and cons of attending Grand Canyon University
body: When I attended GCU they did have a cafeteria and far less fast food options. The problem was everyone was going to the fast food restaurants and hardly anyone went to the cafeteria. So they decided to get rid of it to make room for more restaurants. But I really liked the cafeteria because they had lots of options. Waffles and eggs for breakfast.

author: @GamingSinz

title: pros and cons of attending Grand Canyon University
body: I originally wanted to come to GCU, I went to Discover GCU and fell in love with the campus. I am from Kentucky but I ended up going to a school in Tennessee. The community bathrooms do suck lol, we had 30-40 girls per floor sharing 3-6 stalls and 4-5 showers. We had 3 washers for the whole dorm which was over 100 girls.. The dorms were built in the 19 hundreds so they were very old, we did have a cafeteria but it was worse than high school food it was pretty bad. The student population of the school was around 1,800 and in my English class we had about 10 students lol. When you said less than 100 students I was like wow! That place is huge! Lol
author: @nicolepartin4937

title: why im leaving grand canyon university
body:
I’m really proud of you! That’s a very mature move and there is nothing wrong with the reasons you want to move back home. As a mother of a junior in high school I will even go to the extreme of saying, even if the reason were to move back to be closer to your mom I’d say do it! There’s no shame it that. You tried it, you lived it and you’ve come out a better person for it.
Your wallet and mom will thank you later. Best wishes on your future endeavors at OD. Keep on with the videos too! You’ve got support sweet girl 🤗

title: why im leaving grand canyon university

title: why im leaving grand canyon university

title: why im leaving grand canyon university
body: My daughter is going in the fall 2022. We are Mormon. We are active. I've already spoken with her YSA Bishop. How bad is GCU for members of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints? I am now worried seeing and reading negative stuff.
author: @true.story54

title: why im leaving grand canyon university
body: I saw your video years ago when you first uploaded and I wish I would’ve watched or paid more attention when you explained your situation. I’m now 2 years into GCU and I absolutely hate my experience. I haven’t made any friends and I’ve already had to withdrawal TWICE. let’s not forget that they charge you for every minor inconvenience. So far I’ve spent over a grand on miscellaneous charges due to their unclear policies. Not a great school for first generation students like myself at all. Their on campus student resources suck too. The only reason I’m continuing my education there is because it would be next to impossible to switch over all my credits without having to retake courses and I so desperately wanted to get out of my current home state that I’m manipulating myself into thinking that it’s worth the headache I’ve had to go with, at this school. Education wise, the teachers are great, I feel as though I’ve learned a lot, but I can easily get the same education without all the drama online. I’m glad you found your way out of this school.
author: @777sylviaaa

title: why im leaving grand canyon university
body: I went to ASU 20 years ago and I never felt like I had friends either. Not until my last year.

title: Best Online Psychology Degree Programs (SNHU vs Purdue Global vs Grand Canyon vs Capella University)
body: I attend SNHU online. As a Veteran SNHU accepted 54 credits from my residential and nonresidential courses and schools I attended in the Corps. I am a Psychology Major and doing great. I’m in my 4th course Abnormal Psych and so appreciative for my Professors and classmates. I work as a case manager for the Veteran Affairs and the education that I have received has improved my quality of life so much. I just turned 50. I did not graduate with my class in high school cause I had to go to summer school. After my time in the Corps I graduated from MMI as a Harley and Yamaha tech. Long story short back in school for a new career. It’s never too late and you can accomplish anything you as long as you work hard. By the way SNHU also partners with Sophia Learning where I am taking the rest of my gen eds and focus on my fun core psych classes and save $$$ Stay Driven Fortuna Favet Fortibus.
author: @julianjaramillo2348

body: I graduated from GCU in the master's program. I did very well, realizing this was the beginning of the doctoral program and feeling overwhelmed by the instructor's bias, something we can see and feel. We just need a fair opportunity to display talent based on knowledge and comprehension, not race. Thanks for sharing.

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: I think you're great! You are handsome and you should never be skeptical about showing your skin color! Skin color should never be a factor I mean, it's just a skin color! It's such a small thing, that people have such a problem with. Seriously, how you are treated and treat others is of more significance than skin color. I simply hate that color is even a factor in anything. Keep doing what you do my friend! Find a place where you feel that this isn't a problem! May you have all the great things in life!
author: @csrjustice

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body:
As a concerned parent, I feel compelled to share my thoughts about my daughter’s transition into her freshman year at GCU. It has been a challenging time for her, and I can’t help but feel that the instructors are not making this journey any easier.

I never wanted to believe that she might be targeted, but I can't ignore the troubling signs. It’s disheartening to think that we are still facing limitations based on skin color. My daughter has been working incredibly hard to keep up with the rigorous standards set by her professors, yet it feels like her efforts are not being recognized or supported.

I stand with all of you who are advocating for change. This situation is truly unfortunate, and I hope we can address it together to ensure that every student receives the fair treatment they deserve. Thank you for sharing.
author: @Completehealingcpr19

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: Im black and I go here as an undergrad. Full time student living on campus. You are not alone. Gcu has a problem.
author: @katieburton7725

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: Hello Rick. Thank you for your video and I must say that I am so sorry for your experience that you had at GCU. I decided to go back to school and I came across GCU and applied and was accepted today. The process took about 3 days I believe. I also applied to SNHU at the same time and they accepted me the very next day. So, hearing your story and all the comments below about GCU just blew me away . Not one really good comment . I'm suppose to start at GCU on Monday.
author: @centerfoldpeyton

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: I’m sorry this happened to you and hopefully you were able to involve a lawyer. I did sue a school for a situation and they had to pay my student debt off. I hope and pray you get justice.
author: @OfficialNovey

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: Thank you for posting this. I’m currently looking to transfer from ULL in Louisiana because of discrimination and lack of communication towards disabled veterans. I’ve also been discriminated against at other colleges because I’m Native American, so I’ve been doing extensive research on where I’ll transfer to. There were people at ULL that made me love it first and made me proud to go there (online) and I almost wanted to transfer to in-person, but there are too many red flags popping up in their system. GCU almost sold me with their marketing but I decided to check personal YouTube channels, thankfully.
author: @FrozenVeteranAK

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: That's crazy I have never put a picture up because of that. I hate we have to live like this. I am in my final class at GCU. I was thinking to get in the doctoral program there, but after watching this. I will be looking else where. Thanks.
author: @MiltonStone-x6p

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: Thanks for sharing. I will not be attending there for my masters. What a lot of people don't understand is that this is very common for professors to do it sad because people just want to further their education and get ahead

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: Sadly, I had the very same experience. I finished GCU with a EdS degree instead of a doctorate. I got tired of pouring out money for the mistakes they claimed I was making. Their doctorate program is extremely misleading. Just research your options and transfer to university with integrity in building student achievement for doctoral students.
author: @mligon9612

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: I was looking to attend for my BSN I’m terrified now ugh I was typing in GCU and your video popped up
author: @Its_twinspin

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: I’m from Saginaw, Michigan and I live in Glendale, Arizona. Congratulations on your AMAZING ACCOMPLISHMENT!! My goal is to get my Bachelor’s degree first and to get my Master’s degree in Psychology with an emphasis on Therapy (maybe it’s different). So what you saying is to only do online classes if I’m black, GOT IT.
author: @ImAngieblackwell

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body:
I've graduated from GCU in December 2022. On Friday when I was at work, my sister called me on my lunch break. My sister tells me that Lehman college (CUNY) told my sister that GCU is an accredited institution, however the status said candidacy, which the program major is a candidacy and that Lehman college (CUNY) won't accept my sister, since the program major my sister is taking is a candidacy.
When my sister told me that on Friday, I was shell shocked. GCU is a for-profit college where you have to pay amount of student loans. These for-profit colleges are more like businesses, and would take advantage of students that live in low income housing neighborhoods. And these for-profit colleges would try to get students in the doors. I have to take accountability when I didn’t do my research on the school itself. Please don’t make the same mistake that me and my sister did.
author: @keciaaskew5166

title: Why I Left Grand Canyon University | GCU Review
body: Try going to A.T. Still University. There are no residency requirements for their doctoral program. The tuition is moderate for the doctor of health sciences program.  However, the health administration program is expensive. Check it out online.
author: @saleemahmuhammad6656

title: Everything I ate today as a college student at GCU
body: Keep pushing through those last few weeks, you're almost there! Your videos really motivate me to stay consistent with my workouts.

title: Everything I ate today as a college student at GCU
body: Congratulations graduating for college, you never know where life will take you anywhere, anytime, anywhen, anyday, anyhow. Good Luck after college

title: Everything I ate today as a college student at GCU
body: Your videos inspire me, especially the food intake. I will be like okay 3 meals a day at least, and it has worked. Gotta start working out again. 👌🏾Early congratulations on being almost done. Take everything in!
author: @tiyanawilliams5070

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was in psych lock down 2 weeks ago:  no phone or computer, no fresh air, no sunshine, no therapy offered, I felt like I came out worse than I went in, and I was suicidal.  Things MUST change.  Our society is failing us.
author: @zengwenyoga2894

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: To Rob…I remember first hand the feeling of visiting my wife in the psych ward and then going home to two young kids. In a couple of weeks, we celebrate 48 years. Hang tough, man. She’s an impressive lady.
author: @rcp2212

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: You're very brave to put yourself out there when you're in such a vulnerable position. Be proud of that, not many people could do it.
author: @ronr6450

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It is shocking how different psych wards around the world are. I'm in Germany and here you are not supposed to be all alone in your room. You get a routine to follow. And this routine contains the gym, group therapy, single therapy, arts, meditation and other activities. There is a lot of talking between patients and the staff. Its like a controlled holiday.
author: @larakorrat

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I have been admitted 3 times to a phych ward  some of the nicest most supportive people i ever met were patients
author: @MissMadiGD

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: How difficult and painful it is to live with mental disorders. Those who do not have them will never understand what hell it is.
author: @purpur7187

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Im Nurse who worked on Psychic Ward for year s. You see so many cases from Drug Addiction, Mental illness so much more. God BLESS your Journey
author: @melanieeggers9667

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: She's so full of life and very articulate. Schizophrenia is such a cruel disease. I wish her nothing but the best.😊
author: @mike4619

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been in a psych ward over ten times during my 39 years of life. I'm haven't been in a psych ward since April of 2007. Which is awesome and I'm so proud of myself for that.
author: @Bubbles1984

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I was held for 5 days involuntarily after visiting the ER for panic attacks and horrible anxiety I've battled my whole life. Never once saw a doctor and never recieved a single medication including the ones I was prescribed. No clothing, no phones, no outdoor time, no visitors, and the worst part was I just had oral surgery and had lost all my teeth due to a bad infection in my jaw bone which needed a bone graft so I was unable to eat because they had no accommodation for that diet. Let's just say that's a birthday I will never forget. When asking for meds, the nurse pulled my file and there was an intake sheet with a picture of a 14 year old female in it ( I am a 32 year old male). They couldnt even handle the correct documents to assist in any sort of help.  Still to this day I have ptsd due to my stay.
author: @TheOneTheOnlyKJ

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
My mom was in psych wards multiple times. The last time, I warned the nurses that she was lying and just putting on a face of someone that was “fine” and was there for no reason. I told them to not listen to anything she was saying and the nurses said that they would tell the doctors. She was out and back home two day later.. Exactly ONE week later, she committed suicide!
My mom was always the person that everyone wanted to be around. She was beautiful, kind, intelligent, and the life of the party. NO ONE expected her to do what she did EXCEPT her kids. My brothers and I seen this side of her every day. It was very hard listening to the side that everyone else got because she had been saying for five years that this was what she was going to do😢
Miss you mom❤
author: @squirrelsquad3.0

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
I am in a YouTube rabbit hole right now. I do not experience anything remotely like what you are living with. I admire the hell out of how you have chosen to beautify and help our world. You are a strong and courageous woman.
Thank you.
author: @Razedot

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I've been committed about 15 times. I've finally found a balance with therapy and medication. It's been hell, but I keep chugging along.
author: @mr.bipolar3344

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This is destroying me! For the last 3 years my 62 year old husband of 35 years had a psychotic break. It reached a point where he is committed by the state. He was starving himself. I know he’s sitting there everyday, just sitting. Keep running little girl, keep fighting, the people who love you need you to fight.
author: @Radar2727

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
this is a freaking 5 star hotel.....the place I went...you can not go outside.... cant have phones, cant talk to the other sex, also we could not even be alone any where....wow. I know this is a year old, but you are a strong person and a beautiful soul.

Edit* Thank you for all the likes.
author: @dalekkgames5430

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Spent 40+ years in and out of psychiatric hospitals until we discovered I had a biological problem, not a psychological one. Changed my diet and changed my life. Been mentally free for over 10 years now.
author: @66sprint

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body:
When I was in my late 20s I admitted myself into a psych hospital for suicidal thoughts and because I was experiencing things in my life that I did not know how to handle.
At the time, the hospital was sort of a safe haven for me. At the time it’s what I needed for support of what I was going through.
Sometimes people just need that hospital for things they’re going through.
Wishing you the best.
author: @crystalclear6660

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: I'm an american, but I live in the UK. I was in the psyc ward for 3 months in full-blown psychosis. They were really good to me & the patients were great. I didn't eat for the first month but the food was actually really good. I was on a 5150 in my teens and the psyc ward in the states...it was the most lonely depressing time of my life. The conditions make a huge difference.
author: @GinaRossana-hl6ph

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Last week I went to the ER and someone was having a some sort of psychiatric break. She was clearly terrified and screaming. Seeing the way staff treated her was heartbreaking. I kept hearing her screaming “get off of me” and hearing the nurses giggling about “the pipes on that chick” and “it’s so and sos favorite patient”. It was very disheartening.
author: @meaganhiller3029

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: It always shocks me how different psych wards can be in different places. I was admitted to a psychiatric institution involuntarily and was there for 2 weeks. I had a roommate, we weren't allowed outside of our room from 9pm-7am and weren't allowed back in our room from 7am-7pm. Eating breakfast, lunch, and dinner was mandatory and we all had to be present in the cafeteria, no one was allowed to stay back. We had to attend group sessions to talk about battling mental illness and coping mechanisms. It wasn't very effective. We had a TV in the common area, but they wouldn't turn it on unless it was to watch a movie. I spent all of my free time reading and coloring. We weren't allowed to go outside and only had one window in the whole ward. It literally felt like prison.
author: @halidyer5595

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: This was both hard to watch and incredible to watch.  My extreme admiration goes out to you for documenting something so difficult.  People casually talk of "mental health" as this amorphous concept.  Your bravery in putting yourself out there and attaching a face to a facet of it is extraordinary.  There are so many different struggles people go through.  It's painful seeing someone having to live like that on a daily basis.  I hope you realize just how much of a good you're doing by showing everyone that you can put one foot in front of the other, and that there is no stigma, only a struggle that people have to face down regularly, which takes a lot of strength.  Thank you so much for what you show us all.
author: @revcliff

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: You are so self-aware, Lauren. It’s wonderful that you can understand what’s going on when you’re feeling so troubled right now. You are in my prayers.❤️🙏✨
author: @amynock540

title: A Day in My Life as a Psych Ward Hospital Patient | VLOG
body: Sleep is so important for any patient.   We've known that forever. Sadly, hospitals in the U.S.  haven't figured that out.  You are very articulate.  Unfortunately, from what I have heard, there is far from enough activities for psychiatric patients.  Patients sitting around is  typical for psychiatric patients.  .
author: @monaoconnell5650

title: A Day in the Life - Firefighter
body: To clear things up, you do not have oxygen in your scba tank.  It's compressed air.  Oxygen is highly flammable and would not be a good idea to run in a burning building with a bomb strapped to your back.  Other than that, great video.

title: A Day in the Life - Firefighter
body:
I'm from Liverpool in the UK but I'd just like to say thank you for the brilliant work you all do under extreme conditions,
The UK fire department is getting dragged through the mud currently by this government who know nothing in the field of fire fighting and I feel so sad that the people at the top are treating them like dirt even though this government have slashed our fire service to the bone.
I'm certainly no fireman I haven't got the balls big enough to do it but respect to you all.

title: Everything i ate today as a college student at GCU
body: When I was at ASU I had the cheapest meal plan (7 meals a week) and it also came with $150 maroon & gold dollars on my student card and I'd use it to buy an Einstein bagel and a Jamba juice most days. I'd also steal bananas and apples from the dining hall to survive off of between meals 🤣 i paid my way through college myself. Lost some pounds but it wasn't that bad!
